categories.name: Robinhood Chain Meme
categories.content: Meme coins native to Robinhood Chain.

categories.name: Decentralized Social Media (DeSOC)
categories.content: Decentralized Social Media (DeSOC) refers to social media platforms and protocols that operate without a central authority over user data, content, or infrastructure.

categories.name: Quest-to-Earn
categories.content: Quest-to-Earn refers to a gamified system in which users complete specific tasks or challenges to earn rewards, which can be in the form of points, tokens, or other incentives.

categories.name: Decentralized Identifier (DID)
categories.content: Decentralized Identifier (DID) allows individuals or entities to create and manage their own identifiers independently of any centralized registry, identity provider, or intermediary.

categories.name: Fighting Games
categories.content: Fighting game centered around combat between two or more number of characters.

categories.name: Yield Tokenization Product
categories.content: Yield tokenization product refers to converting the expected future yield or returns from an asset into tradable tokens on a blockchain, allowing for the trading, management, and optimization of yield independently from the underlying asset.

categories.name: Quantum-Resistant
categories.content: Quantum-resistant projects are characterised by their use of post-quantum cryptographic algorithms (e.g., hash-based, lattice-based) to remain secure against potential attacks from future quantum computers.

categories.name: Four.meme Ecosystem (BNB Memes)
categories.content: Four.meme Ecosystem consists of tokens launched via four.meme on BNB Chain.

categories.name: Wrapped-Tokens
categories.content: Wrapped tokens offer a way to circumvent the lack of interoperability between different blockchains. The wrapping of a token happens when the original asset is put into a “wrapper” and deployed on a different blockchain. With it being wrapped, the cryptocurrency can now be used on a blockchain where it previously wasn’t available. One example of a wrapped token would be Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC). It is an ERC-20 version of Bitcoin, which allows it to be used on protocols based on Ethereum. Watch this video to learn more about Wrapped tokens.

categories.name: BagsApp Ecosystem
categories.content: BagsApp Ecosystem consists of tokens launched via bags.fm.

categories.name: JPY Stablecoin
categories.content: A JPY stablecoin is a type of cryptocurrency that is designed to maintain a stable value by being pegged to the Japanese yen.

categories.name: Orynth Ecosystem
categories.content: Projects launched via Orynth, designed to let users trade on the attention and interest surrounding early-stage software products and MVPs.

categories.name: Zero Knowledge (ZK)
categories.content:
Zero-knowledge proof or Zero-knowledge protocol is a method by which one party (the prover) can prove to another party (the verifier) that a given statement is true while the prover avoids conveying any additional information apart from the fact that the statement is indeed true.

This category tracks cryptocurrency that utilises Zero Knowledge implementation to execute transactions, which may include smart contract execution, value transfer, and others.

categories.name: Regenerative Finance (ReFi)
categories.content: Regenerative finance (ReFi) aims to create economic systems that go beyond generating financial returns to focus on restoring and enhancing social, environmental, and economic well-being.

categories.name: Privacy Coins
categories.content: Cryptocurrencies designed primarily to function as digital cash or a store of value. Their architecture prioritizes the anonymity of the sender, receiver, and amount. They do not support general-purpose smart contracts or a dApp ecosystem.

categories.name: Arcade Games
categories.content: Classic-style games with simple mechanics, high replayability, and score-based progression.

categories.name: Chinese Meme
categories.content: Chinese meme coins are inspired by China memes, cultural elements, or internet phenomena.

categories.name: BTCfi Protocol
categories.content: BTCfi Protocol refers to DeFi protocols built on or around the Bitcoin ecosystem. It aims to bring DeFi activities such as lending, borrowing, and yield farming to Bitcoin holders.

categories.name: Tourism
categories.content: A coin or token that leverages blockchain technology to improve various aspects of the tourism industry.

categories.name: Cat-Themed
categories.content: Meme coins which are inspired by cat.
